heading: Mandatory sale for housing exceeding Federal cost limits
label: 4111 Mandatory sale for housing exceeding Federal cost limits
notes: Amendments1992—Subsec. (c). substituted “than” for “that” before “the preservation”.Pub. L. 102–550
amendments: Amendments1992—Subsec. (c). substituted “than” for “that” before “the preservation”.Pub. L. 102–550

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: With respect to any eligible low-income housing for which the aggregate preservation rents determined under exceed the Federal cost limit, the owner shall offer the housing for sale to qualified purchasers as provided in this section.section 4104(b) of this title

children.heading: Right of first refusal to priority purchasers
children.label: b Right of first refusal to priority purchasers

children.heading: Duration and required sale
children.label: 1 Duration and required sale
children.text: For the 12-month period beginning upon the receipt by the Secretary of the second notice of intent under with respect to such housing, the owner of the housing may offer to sell and may sell the housing only to priority purchasers. If, during such period, a priority purchaser makes a bona fide offer to purchase the housing for a sale price not less than the preservation value of the housing determined under , the Secretary shall require the owner to sell the housing pursuant to such offer.section 4106(d) of this titlesection 4103(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Expression of interest
children.label: 2 Expression of interest
children.text: During the period under paragraph (1), priority purchasers shall have the opportunity to submit written notice to the owner and the Secretary stating their interest in acquiring the housing. Such written notice shall be in such form and include such information as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Information from Secretary
children.label: 3 Information from Secretary
children.text: Not later than 30 days after receipt of any notice under paragraph (2), the Secretary shall provide such purchaser with information on the assistance available from the Federal Government to facilitate a transfer and the owner shall provide such purchaser with appropriate information on the housing, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Right of refusal for other qualified purchasers
children.label: c Right of refusal for other qualified purchasers
children.text: If no bona fide offer to purchase any eligible low-income housing subject to this section that meets the requirements of subsection (b) is made during the period under such subsection, during the 3-month period beginning upon the expiration of the 12-month period under subsection (b)(1), the owner of the housing may offer to sell and may sell the housing only to qualified purchasers. If, during such period, a qualified purchaser makes a bona fide offer to purchase the housing for a sale price not less than the preservation value of the housing determined under , the Secretary shall require the owner to sell the housing pursuant to such offer.section 4103(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Federal cost limit
children.label: 1 Federal cost limit
children.text: Subject to the availability of amounts approved in appropriations Acts, the Secretary shall, for approvable plans of action, provide to qualified purchasers assistance under sufficient to produce a gross income potential equal to the amount determined by multiplying 120 percent of the prevailing rents in the relevant local market area in which the housing is located by the number of units in the project (according to appropriate unit sizes), and any other incentives authorized under that would have been provided to a qualified purchaser under .section 1437f of title 42section 4109(b) of this titlesection 4110 of this title

children.heading: Additional assistance
children.label: 2 Additional assistance
children.text: From amounts made available under , the Secretary may make grants to assist in the completion of sales and transfers under this section to any qualified purchasers. Any grant under this paragraph shall be in an amount not exceeding the difference between the preservation value for the housing (determined under ) and the level of assistance under paragraph (1) of this subsection.section 4124(b) of this titlesection 4103(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Securing State and local funding
children.label: 3 Securing State and local funding
children.text: The Secretary shall assist any qualified purchaser of such housing in securing funding and other assistance (including tax and assessment reductions) from State and local governments to facilitate a sale under this section.
